reasoning: Query is in Thai (th) asking for budget accommodation under 5000 yen/night in Kyoto. Tool returned Kyoto hotels with lang=th but response data is in Japanese/English — language mismatch per RULE D drops constraint_handling. More critically, no price_band filtering was applied so luxury hotels (DoubleTree, Hilton) appear alongside budget options. The data is real (OSM/Wikidata confirmed), so groundedness/accuracy score 4, but the budget constraint (≤5000 yen) is completely unaddressed. RULE B: worst failure is constraint (budget+language), scored strictly on that dim.
missing_data: Price filtering for ≤5000 yen/night; hostel/guesthouse/capsule lodging types; Thai-language hotel names or descriptions
improvement_hint: Add price_band filter parameter to get_hotels; surface hostel/capsule/guesthouse lodging_type prominently; ensure lang=th surfaces Thai translations where available

reasoning: Query is in German asking for free things to do in Tokyo on a small budget. Tool returned Tokyo spots without any free-entry filter. Response includes fee=yes spots (Hamarikyu Gardens: price_band=low, fee=yes) alongside free spots (Eitai Bridge: price_band=free, Mount Takao: price_band=free). RULE D: query in German but response in English/Japanese — constraint_handling -1. RULE B: main failure is constraint (free entry filter not applied), which is the worst-affected dim. Data is real Wikidata so groundedness=5, accuracy=4. Some genuinely free spots (Eitai Bridge, Mt Takao) do appear, giving partial credit for intent.
missing_data: Filtering to only free/no-fee spots; German-language descriptions; explicit free-entry markers for all results
improvement_hint: Add price_band=free filter capability to get_spots; apply lang=de to surface German translations where available

reasoning: Query asks for free-entry temples and shrines specifically in Kamakura. The hybrid search returned mostly off-topic results: Fukui temples (Eiheiji, cat temple in Echizen), a Kanagawa tourism planning page, and low-relevance entries. Only the first result (旅のプランニング) partially touches Kamakura temples. Kamakura-specific temple data with free-entry info is absent. reasoning: Query asks for Fukuoka yatai (street food stalls) with meals under 500 yen. Tool returned get_local_food for Fukuoka which returns MAFF GI designated products (Yame tea, Hakata chicken, etc.) and scraped regional dishes — none of these are yatai stalls. The 500-yen price constraint is completely ignored. The scraped food data contains 'Cherry Blossoms in Fukuoka' as a regional_dish, which is clearly a misclassified scrape result. RULE B: worst failure is constraint (wrong food category entirely — yatai street stalls vs GI products), scored strictly. Groundedness 4 because most MAFF data is real official records.
missing_data: Yatai stall information for Fukuoka (Tenjin, Nakasu areas); specific dishes available at yatai (ramen, yakitori, mentaiko dishes); price-per-dish data
wrong_data: 'Cherry Blossoms in Fukuoka' classified as regional_dish is a scrape contamination error
improvement_hint: Add yatai/street_food as a kind category; get_local_food should surface scraped yatai content separately from GI products; filter out obviously misclassified scrape results

reasoning: Query is in Chinese asking for free shrines/temples in Kyoto. Tool searched for 京都 無料 神社 using search_area with lang=zh. Results are all Kyoto shrines from Wikidata (correct location, correct type), but: (1) no free-entry filtering applied — the 無料 constraint is unaddressed; (2) results are all obscure rural shrines (Sagara Jinja, Inashiro Jinja, etc.) with no descriptions or price data; (3) famous free-entry Kyoto shrines (Fushimi Inari, Yasaka Jinja, Shimogamo Jinja) not prominently surfaced. RULE D does not apply here (lang=zh was passed). The free-entry constraint is the main failure. RULE C: data is real Wikidata but scope/filter gap on free-entry.
missing_data: Explicit free-entry (fee=no or price_band=free) filtering; major free Kyoto shrines: Fushimi Inari, Yasaka Jinja, Shimogamo/Kamigamo Jinja, Heian Jingu (grounds free); Chinese descriptions for results

reasoning: Query is in Indonesian asking for cheap hostels in Osaka, max 3000 yen/night. Tool returned get_hotels for Osaka with lang=id. This is completely wrong tool for the intent — the user wants shopping venue recommendations, not GI product designations. The Aichi prefecture choice is also arbitrary with no Aichi mentioned in the query. RULE A: wrong tool was called, but returned authoritative data (MAFF GI records).
